[218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 出于爱私 - Compound phrase meaning 'arising from private affection,' used to describe a motivation influenced by personal feelings.
  • 虽资不残酷 - Means 'though not cruel in nature,' indicating a character trait that is not inherently harsh.
  • 以圣智自处 - Means 'to regard oneself as wise and sagacious,' suggesting a self-perception of intellectual superiority.
  • 少为吏时 - Refers to the period when one was a young official, indicating a specific stage in one's career.
  • 全所教用术取人 - Means 'to employ tactics to select people,' describing a methodical approach to choosing individuals based on certain criteria.
  • 外示寛宏以取名 - Means 'to outwardly display generosity and magnanimity to gain fame,' indicating a superficial display of positive qualities for public recognition.
  • 内实淫纵自肆 - Means 'internally, indulging in licentiousness and unrestrained behavior,' revealing a hidden, immoral side.
  • 讳言过恶 - Means 'to avoid mentioning faults and misdeeds,' indicating a tendency to conceal errors.
  • 喜听谀言 - Means 'to enjoy listening to flattering words,' suggesting a preference for flattery.
  • 闇于用人 - Means 'to be obscure or unclear in the use of people,' indicating a lack of clarity in personnel management.
  • 其将相止取从来贵戚 - Means 'to appoint only those who are of noble lineage,' indicating a bias in selecting high-ranking officials.
  • 虽不杀大臣 - Means 'though not killing high officials,' suggesting a policy of non-lethality towards important figures.
  • 其骄将多难制不驯 - Means 'its arrogant generals are difficult to control and manage,' indicating a challenge in managing military leaders.
  • 况不知大畧 - Means 'how much more so, lacking an understanding of the big picture,' emphasizing a lack of strategic insight.
  • 临大事辄退怯自沮 - Means 'facing major events, they retreat and become discouraged,' indicating a tendency to give up in the face of significant challenges.
  • 此所以一遇勍敌而不能振也 - Means 'this is why, when encountering a strong enemy, they cannot rally,' explaining a failure to respond effectively to strong opposition.
  • 大抵金国之政杂用辽宋法令 - Means 'generally, the Jin state's governance mixed the laws of Liao and Song,' indicating a blend of legal systems.
  • 所以支持百年 - Means 'thus supporting the state for a century,' suggesting the effectiveness of this mixed system.
  • 然其分别汉人且不变家政 - Means 'however, its treatment of Han Chinese and its household policies remained unchanged,' indicating a persistent issue.
  • 不得士大夫心 - Means 'it did not win the hearts of scholars and officials,' suggesting a lack of support from the intellectual elite.
  • 此所以不能长久 - Means 'this is why it could not last long,' explaining the eventual downfall.
  • 向使大定后宣孝得立 - Means 'if, after the reign of Emperor Xuanzong, Emperor Shunzong had ascended the throne,' indicating a hypothetical scenario.
  • 尽行中国法 - Means 'to fully implement Chinese law,' suggesting a shift towards a more centralized legal system.
  • 明昌承安间复知保守整顿以防后忧 - Means 'during the Mingchang and Cheng'an periods, there was a renewed effort to preserve and整顿 to prevent future troubles,' indicating a period of reform.
  • 南渡之后能内修政令以恢复为志 - Means 'after the southern relocation, if they had focused on internal reforms and aimed to restore the state,' suggesting a potential path to recovery.
  • 则其国祚亦未必遽絶也 - Means 'then the state's longevity might not have been cut short,' indicating a possible alternative outcome.
  • 尝记泰和间有云中李纯甫由小官上书万言 - Means 'I recall that during the Taihe period, Li Chunfu from Yunzhong, a minor official, submitted a ten-thousand-word memorial,' indicating a historical reference.
  • 大畧以为此政当有为日 - Means 'roughly stating that this governance should have a period of action,' suggesting a call for active governance.
  • 当路以为迂阔笑之 - Means 'those in power regarded it as impractical and laughed at it,' indicating a lack of support for the proposal.
  • 宴安自处以至土崩瓦 - Means 'living in ease and comfort until the state collapsed,' indicating a decline due to complacency.
  • 南渡后复有以机 - Means 'after the southern relocation, there were again those who used cunning,' suggesting a continuation of manipulative practices.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金国 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty, a major power in northern China during the 12th and 13th centuries.
  • 辽宋 - Refers to the Liao and Song Dynasties, the neighboring states whose legal systems influenced the Jin.
  • 大定后宣孝 - Refers to the reigns of Emperor Xuanzong and Emperor Shunzong of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 明昌承安 - Refers to the reign periods of Emperor Shunzong, specifically the Mingchang and Cheng'an eras.
  • 南渡 - Refers to the southern relocation of the Jin Dynasty's capital, a significant historical event.
  • 泰和 - Refers to the Taihe period, a specific era during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 云中李纯甫 - Refers to Li Chunfu, a historical figure from Yunzhong who submitted a memorial during the Taihe period.
  • 士大夫 - Refers to the scholar-official class, a key group in Chinese society and governance.

Proper Nouns

  • 金国 - The Jin Dynasty.
  • 辽宋 - The Liao and Song Dynasties.
  • 大定后宣孝 - Emperor Xuanzong and Emperor Shunzong of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 明昌承安 - The Mingchang and Cheng'an periods of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 南渡 - The southern relocation of the Jin Dynasty's capital.
  • 泰和 - The Taihe period of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 云中李纯甫 - Li Chunfu, a historical figure from Yunzhong.

[219]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 防宜急有备为言者而上下泰然俱不以为心 - Those who should have been on high alert and prepared, yet both high and low were complacent and did not take it to heart.
  • 以至宗庙丘墟家国废絶 - Leading to the desolation of ancestral temples and the collapse of the state.
  • 此古人所谓何世无竒材而遗之草泽者也 - This is what ancient people referred to when they said that every era has extraordinary talents but leaves them in the wilderness.
  • 金银珠玉世人所甚贵 - Gold, silver, jewels, and precious stones are highly valued by the world.
  • 及遇凶年则不及菽粟何哉 - But when faced with a famine, they are not as valuable as beans and grains. Why is that?
  • 事有先后势有缓急也 - There is a sequence to things and a priority in urgency.
  • 平时富贵之家求一珠玉犀玩好器物至发粟出帛惟恐其不得 - In times of peace, wealthy families would go to great lengths to acquire precious items, even to the point of using grain and silk, fearing they might not get them.
  • 将以充其室夸耀于人以自乐者皆是也 - They would use these items to fill their homes, to show off to others, and to enjoy themselves. All of this is true.
  • 壬辰嵗余在大梁时城久被围公私乏食米一升至银二两余殍死者相望人视金银如泥土使用不计 - In the year Renchen, when I was in Daliang, the city had been under siege for a long time, and both public and private food supplies were scarce. A measure of rice cost two taels of silver, and the dead were everywhere. People treated gold and silver as if they were dirt, using them without counting.
  • 士庶之家出其平日珠玉玩好妆具环佩锦绣衣衾日陈于天津桥市中惟博鬻升合米豆以救朝夕 - Both commoners and nobles would bring out their usual jewels, ornaments, and fine clothing and display them daily in the market of Tianjin Bridge, just to buy a little rice and beans to survive from day to day.
  • 尝记余家一毳袍极致宻鲜完博米八升金钗易牛肉一肩趋售之以是知明君贵五谷而贱金玉诚知其本也 - I remember that one of my family's fur coats, which was extremely fine and almost new, was exchanged for eight measures of rice. A golden hairpin was traded for a shoulder of beef. From this, I understand that a wise ruler values grains and despises gold and jade, truly knowing the essence of things.
  • 古人云薪如桂米如珠岂虚言哉 - As the ancients said, firewood is as expensive as cinnamon and rice is as valuable as pearls. Is this not a true statement?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 壬辰嵗 - The year Renchen, a specific year in the Chinese calendar.
  • 大梁 - Daliang, an ancient name for Kaifeng, a city in Henan Province, China.
  • 天津桥 - The Tianjin Bridge, a famous bridge in Luoyang, Henan Province.
  • 毳袍 - A fur coat made of fine animal fur, a luxury item in ancient China.
  • 金钗 - A golden hairpin, a decorative item worn by women.
  • 明君贵五谷而贱金玉 - The idea that a wise ruler values grains and despises gold and jade, emphasizing the importance of practical necessities over luxury items.

Proper Nouns

  • 壬辰嵗 - The year Renchen.
  • 大梁 - Daliang, an ancient name for Kaifeng.
  • 天津桥 - The Tianjin Bridge in Luoyang.
  • 毳袍 - A fur coat made of fine animal fur.
  • 金钗 - A golden hairpin.
  • 明君贵五谷而贱金玉 - The idea that a wise ruler values grains and despises gold and jade.

[220]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文章各有体 - Formal expression meaning that literary works have their own distinct forms or styles.
  • 不可相犯欺 - Means 'cannot be mutually violated or deceived,' suggesting that different styles should not be mixed or misused.
  • 古文不宜蹈袭前人成语 - Means that ancient prose should not blindly imitate previous people's set phrases.
  • 当以竒异自强 - Means to strengthen oneself by being unique and novel.
  • 四六宜用前人成语 - Means that parallel prose (四六文) should use previous people's set phrases.
  • 不宜生涩求异 - Means that it is not advisable to seek novelty at the expense of readability.
  • 如散文不宜用诗家语 - Means that prose should not use poetic language.
  • 诗句不宜用散文言 - Means that poetry should not use prose-like language.
  • 律赋不宜犯散文言 - Means that regulated verse should not use prose-like language.
  • 散文不犯律赋语 - Means that prose should not use regulated verse-like language.
  • 皆判然各异 - Means that they are all clearly different.
  • 如杂用之 - Means that if they are mixed together.
  • 非惟失体 - Means that it is not only to lose the proper style.
  • 且梗目难通 - Means that it is also difficult to understand.
  • 然学者闇于识 - Means that however, scholars are often ignorant of this.
  • 多混乱交出 - Means that they often mix things up.
  • 且互相诋诮 - Means that they also criticize each other.
  • 不自觉知此 - Means that they do not realize this problem.
  • 虽一二名公不免也 - Means that even some famous scholars cannot avoid this.
  • 长于此者必短于彼 - Means that those who excel in one area must be deficient in another.
  • 优于大者或劣于小 - Means that those who are better in the major aspects may be worse in the minor ones.
  • 士君子穷处不能活妻子免饥寒 - Means that scholars and gentlemen in poverty cannot support their families and avoid hunger and cold.
  • 及其得志 - Means when they achieve their ambitions.
  • 则兼济天下 - Means then they can help the whole world.
  • 使民物各得所 - Means to make people and things each find their proper place.
  • 太公困于鼓刀钓渔 - Refers to the story of Jiang Ziya (太公), who was poor and worked as a butcher and fisherman before becoming a famous minister.
  • 伊尹躬耕莘野 - Refers to the story of Yi Yin (伊尹), who worked as a farmer before becoming a famous minister.
  • 彼岂不能妄营财利 - Means that they could not have failed to seek wealth and profit.
  • 使生理优游耶 - Means to live a comfortable life.
  • 耻不为也 - Means that they were ashamed to do so.
  • 若夫韩淮阴少年乞食漂母 - Refers to the story of Han Xin (韩淮阴), who was a poor young man and was helped by a washerwoman (漂母).
  • 人皆笑嗤 - Means that people all laughed at him.
  • 及为将 - Means when he became a general.
  • 料敌制胜无遗策 - Means that he could plan and win battles without any mistakes.
  • 卒能佐汉祖定天下 - Means that he finally helped Emperor Gaozu (汉祖) to establish the Han dynasty.
  • 身享南面之乐 - Means that he enjoyed the pleasure of ruling.
  • 岂昔之拙而今之巧耶 - Means that is it not that he was foolish in the past and clever now?
  • 材有所长 - Means that talents have their strengths.
  • 志有所不为也 - Means that there are things they choose not to do.
  • 因是以思吾侪今日遭大变 - Means that therefore, thinking about our current situation of great changes.
  • 日惟糊口之不给 - Means that we can barely make ends meet.
  • 不免有求于人 - Means that we cannot help but ask others for help.
  • 亦不足怪 - Means that it is not surprising.
  • 但恨不能自渔樵亲耕稼以自给 - Means that we regret not being able to fish, cut wood, and farm to support ourselves.
  • 如古之人耳 - Means like the ancients.
  • 彼穷居妻子有愠言 - Means that they live in poverty and their wives and children complain.
  • 乡人贱人交游笑之 - Means that neighbors, lowly people, and friends all laugh at them.
  • 又何病也 - Means what is the problem with that?
  • 理固然也 - Means that this is a natural thing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 文章各有体 - Refers to the distinct styles and forms of literary works.
  • 古文 - Refers to ancient prose, a style of writing that emphasizes simplicity and directness.
  • 太公 - Refers to Jiang Ziya (姜子牙), a famous minister in Chinese history.
  • 伊尹 - Refers to Yi Yin (伊尹), a famous minister in Chinese history.
  • 韩淮阴 - Refers to Han Xin (韩信), a famous general in Chinese history.
  • 汉祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu (刘邦), the founder of the Han dynasty.
  • 渔樵 - Refers to fishing and cutting wood, activities associated with a simple, rural life.
  • 耕稼 - Refers to farming, another activity associated with a simple, rural life.

Proper Nouns

  • 太公 - Jiang Ziya (姜子牙), a famous minister in Chinese history.
  • 伊尹 - Yi Yin (伊尹), a famous minister in Chinese history.
  • 韩淮阴 - Han Xin (韩信), a famous general in Chinese history.
  • 汉祖 - Emperor Gaozu (刘邦), the founder of the Han dynasty.

[221]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如养木 - Metaphor comparing the nurturing of talent to the cultivation of trees.
  • 干霄拂云之势 - Describes a towering and impressive presence, often used to symbolize great achievements or influence.
  • 括囊袖手 - Means to remain silent and inactive to avoid trouble, often used to describe a cautious or passive approach.
  • 自非坚刚不防之志超世绝伦之人 - Means that unless one has an unyielding and extraordinary spirit, they are unlikely to remain unchanged in the face of adversity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 国家养育人材 - Refers to the state's responsibility in nurturing talent, a common theme in classical Chinese political thought.
  • 明堂太室 - Refers to the grand halls of the ancient Chinese palaces, symbolizing important roles in society.
  • 士大夫 - Refers to scholars and officials, a key social class in classical Chinese society.
  • 屏山之才 - Refers to a specific person or type of talent, possibly a historical figure or a metaphor for exceptional ability.
  • 余先君 - Refers to the speaker's late father, often used to introduce personal or familial perspectives.

Proper Nouns

  • 国家 - The state or nation.
  • 士大夫 - Scholars and officials.
  • 明堂太室 - The grand halls of the ancient Chinese palaces.
  • 屏山 - A specific person or type of talent, possibly a historical figure or a metaphor for exceptional ability.
  • 余先君 - The speaker's late father.

[222]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天定亦能胜人 - Philosophical expression meaning 'Heaven (or fate) can also overcome people,' suggesting that destiny or natural forces can surpass human effort.
  • 人众亦能胜天 - Counter-argument meaning 'People can also overcome Heaven (or fate),' implying that human effort can triumph over natural forces.
  • 盖人气胜也 - Concluding remark meaning 'Because human warmth overcomes it,' explaining why people feel warmer when talking.
  • 大抵有势力者能不为造物所欺 - General statement meaning 'Generally, those with power are not deceived by nature,' suggesting that strength or influence can counteract natural forces.
  • 所以有势者亦造物所使也 - Philosophical conclusion meaning 'And those with power are also instruments of nature,' implying that even power is part of a larger natural order.
  • 有志气之乐 - Refers to the joy derived from achieving goals or aspirations.
  • 有形体之乐 - Refers to physical pleasures, such as comfort and material enjoyment.
  • 有性命之乐 - Refers to spiritual or moral joy, derived from virtues like righteousness and filial piety.
  • 得时者之所有也 - Means 'What those who are in the right time have,' referring to success or opportunity.
  • 富厚者之所备也 - Means 'What those who are wealthy and abundant have,' referring to material possessions.
  • 穷士之所有也 - Means 'What the poor scholar has,' referring to spiritual or moral wealth despite material poverty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 严冬 - Refers to a harsh winter, a common theme in classical Chinese literature symbolizing hardship.
  • 大厦 - Refers to a large building, often used metaphorically to represent a place of refuge or comfort.
  • 事业功名 - Refers to achievements and fame, a key value in Confucian thought.
  • 权势爵位 - Refers to power, authority, and titles, reflecting the hierarchical nature of traditional Chinese society.
  • 酒色衣食 - Refers to indulgences in alcohol, beauty, food, and clothing, representing physical pleasures.
  • 使令车马 - Refers to servants and vehicles, symbolizing wealth and status.
  • 仁义礼乐 - Refers to the core virtues of Confucianism: benevolence, righteousness, propriety, and music.
  • 忠信孝弟 - Refers to loyalty, trustworthiness, filial piety, and fraternal respect, key moral values in Confucian thought.
  • 公卿宰辅 - Refers to high-ranking officials, representing political power and influence.
  • 节义 - Refers to loyalty and righteousness, often associated with historical figures who upheld moral principles.
  • 浮沈闾里 - Refers to living a simple, unremarkable life in the community, reflecting a humble existence.

[223]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 持身但以徳善立名者 - Refers to those who establish their reputation solely through virtue and goodness.
  • 放浪山林草衣木食 - Describes a lifestyle of wandering in the mountains and forests, living a simple life.
  • 以髙潔自居 - Means 'to consider oneself as pure and noble.'
  • 抒心文史 - Refers to expressing one's thoughts through literature and history.
  • 以著述吟讽 - Means 'through writing and recitation.'
  • 聞者 - Refers to those who are known or heard of.
  • 研精技艺 - Means 'to study and refine skills.'
  • 阴阳医药卜筮字画绘画 - Refers to various skills and arts including divination, medicine, calligraphy, and painting.
  • 以名世者 - Means 'to become famous in the world.'
  • 纵酒放歌 - Describes a lifestyle of drinking and singing freely.
  • 拨弃礼法 - Means 'to discard rituals and laws.'
  • 以乐其形体 - Means 'to enjoy their bodies.'
  • 抑情忘欲 - Means 'to suppress emotions and forget desires.'
  • 链身服气 - Refers to practices of physical and spiritual cultivation.
  • 以觊飞升 - Means 'to aspire to ascend to heaven.'
  • 要之各从所好 - Means 'in summary, everyone follows their own preferences.'
  • 且有定数 - Means 'and there is a fixed destiny.'
  • 亦安能一其迹耶 - Means 'how could they all be made uniform?'
  • 幼而苦学 - Means 'studying hard from a young age.'
  • 及于齿壮 - Means 'by the time one reaches adulthood.'
  • 学虽粗成 - Means 'though the learning is somewhat complete.'
  • 未有所遇合 - Means 'without encountering opportunities.'
  • 穷居草野 - Describes a life of poverty and isolation.
  • 日惟衣食之不充 - Means 'every day, just barely having enough food and clothing.'
  • 将为事业功名 - Means 'intending to achieve career and fame.'
  • 而不可得 - Means 'but unable to attain.'
  • 非居位当言 - Means 'not being in a position to speak.'
  • 且临事变 - Means 'and facing changing circumstances.'
  • 可以立节义 - Means 'to establish one's integrity and righteousness.'
  • 愿服链以懒惰 - Means 'wishing to cultivate oneself but being lazy.'
  • 不能放纵以拘窒 - Means 'unable to be free but constrained.'
  • 不喜诸技艺 - Means 'not liking various skills.'
  • 顾后日穷达 - Means 'considering future success or failure.'
  • 犹未可知 - Means 'still uncertain.'
  • 独守吾残编断藁者 - Means 'remaining alone with my incomplete and scattered writings.'
  • 犹未为痴计也 - Means 'is still not a foolish decision.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 放浪山林 - Describes a lifestyle of wandering in the mountains and forests, often associated with Daoist hermits.
  • 草衣木食 - Refers to a simple, natural lifestyle.
  • 抒心文史 - Expressing one's thoughts through literature and history.
  • 阴阳医药卜筮字画绘画 - Refers to various skills and arts, including divination, medicine, calligraphy, and painting.
  • 纵酒放歌 - Describes a lifestyle of drinking and singing freely, often associated with the literati.
  • 拨弃礼法 - Refers to discarding rituals and laws, often associated with Daoist or Confucian ideals.
  • 抑情忘欲 - Refers to practices of suppressing emotions and desires, often associated with Daoist or Buddhist practices.
  • 链身服气 - Refers to practices of physical and spiritual cultivation, often associated with Daoist practices.
  • 觊飞升 - Aspiring to ascend to heaven, often associated with Daoist or Buddhist beliefs.
  • 经籍文翰 - Refers to classical texts and literature.
  • 穷达 - Refers to success or failure in one's career and life.

Proper Nouns

  • No proper nouns in the passage.

[224]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 陪诸举子进取矣 - have accompanied scholars in their pursuit of advancement.
  • 偕诸朋友讲学矣 - have studied with friends.
  • 视诸农夫耕获矣 - have observed farmers plowing and harvesting.
  • 同诸少年嬉逰矣 - have played with young people.
  • 诣诸王公贵人干谒矣 - have visited nobles and officials to seek favors.
  • 上为卿相行轻济之谋 - at the highest level, I have served as a minister and engaged in light and swift strategies.
  • 下为仆吏执奔走之役 - at the lower level, I have served as a low-ranking official and performed menial tasks.
  • 其于世故无所不涉 - I have encountered all kinds of worldly affairs.
  • 今而时值乱离 - now, in times of chaos and separation.
  • 屏居故山之下 - live in seclusion in my hometown.
  • 回思向者之事 - reflect on past events.
  • 扰扰胶胶于身 - troubled and entangled in my life.
  • 如梦觉如酒醒 - like waking from a dream or becoming sober.
  • 不见纤毫形迹 - without seeing any trace or sign.
  • 区区虑衣食之不充 - preoccupied with the worry of insufficient food and clothing.
  • 惧志意之不得 - fear of not achieving one's aspirations.
  • 乐天知命坎止流行 - to be content with fate, know one's destiny, and flow with the current.
  • 与万物同始终 - to share the beginning and end with all things.
  • 其学之不至也 - it is due to the lack of learning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卿相 - high-ranking officials or ministers in ancient China.
  • 仆吏 - low-ranking officials or servants.
  • 世故 - worldly affairs or experiences, often with a negative connotation.
  • 乱离 - chaotic and separated times, often associated with war or political turmoil.
  • 故山 - one's hometown or native place.
  • 乐天知命 - a philosophical concept from Confucianism and Daoism, emphasizing contentment with one's fate and acceptance of destiny.
  • 坎止流行 - a metaphorical expression from Daoism, suggesting going with the flow of life and adapting to circumstances.

[225]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尚 - Indicates admiration or preference for something.
  • 間 - Means 'among' or 'in the midst of.'
  • 不為風俗所移者 - Literally means 'those not moved by customs,' referring to individuals who remain steadfast despite societal influences.
  • 或以道徳顯 - Means 'some are known for their morality.'
  • 或以節行聞 - Means 'some are renowned for their integrity and conduct.'
  • 或以智量稱 - Means 'some are praised for their wisdom and capacity.'
  • 或以風義着 - Means 'some are celebrated for their righteousness and virtue.'
  • 卓爾不羣 - A compound phrase meaning 'outstanding and unique.'
  • 豪杰之士 - Refers to heroic or distinguished individuals.
  • 道藏 - The Daoist canon of scriptures.
  • 練石服氣 - Practices involving refining stones and breathing exercises in Daoism.
  • 長生登仙 - Refers to achieving immortality and ascending to the heavens.
  • 役使鬼神 - Commanding spirits and deities.
  • 佛书 - Buddhist scriptures.
  • 天堂地狱 - Heaven and hell in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 因果轮回 - The concept of karma and rebirth in Buddhism.
